data.name: Grassroots Organizing Assistant AI Framework v2.1 (Full Beta Version)
data.description:
System Overview

This Creative Commons framework provides sophisticated organizing intelligence with built-in progressive principles and proven methodological integration. Designed for labor campaigns, issue-based organizing, electoral work, and coalition building.

 

Core Capabilities

Strategic Intelligence Commands:

  • Opposition research and vulnerability analysis
  • Coalition building with power mapping
  • Volunteer mobilization and capacity assessment
  • Fundraising strategy tied to organizing outcomes
  • Narrative development using proven frameworks
  • Rapid response protocols for crisis situations

Labor-Specific Tools:

  • Strike readiness assessment using McAlevey methodology
  • Whole worker organizing vs. mobilizing model analysis
  • Contract campaign strategy and escalation planning
  • Labor-community coalition development

Advanced Features:

  • Cultural competency integration for diverse organizing contexts
  • 198 Methods of Nonviolent Resistance tactical library
  • Constitutional progressive principles validation throughout
  • Movement security protocols and infiltration protection
     

Technical Specifications

Platform Compatibility: Optimized for ChatGPT, Claude, and open-source models
Token Efficiency: 2,400 tokens base framework, modular expansion available
Deployment: Copy-paste implementation, no technical infrastructure required
Updates: Continuous improvement based on field testing and organizer feedback

Progressive Principles Architecture:

  • Anti-racism validation integrated into all command templates
  • Economic equity assessment for strategic recommendations
  • Anti-oppression frameworks prevent harmful tactical suggestions
  • Community Toolbox integration for vetted movement resources

 

Support Liberation Labs: This framework is freely available through Creative Commons licensing. If your organization finds value in these tools, please consider a donation to support continued development and maintenance. Suggested contributions based on organizational capacity: $50/month (budget under $100k), $150/month (budget $100k-$500k), $300/month (budget over $500k).
